description: Per-vector conformance expectations for the TSP v3.0 fixture suite. Check names (schema, contentHash, ledgerHash, signatures) and statuses (passed/failed/skipped) match the reference verifier's verifyLocal() vocabulary. Each vector exercises a distinct normative property; see 'kind'.

vectors.note: Reference signed envelope (instance key). Full local verify must pass.
ADR-0002: a signed envelope carrying executionProvenance verifies end-to-end.
ADR-0002 granular failure profile: provenance mutated AFTER signing fails ledgerHash + signature, NOT schema. This is the normative distinction the domain rule guarantees.
The stored content.hash is a representational placeholder; what is normative is that sha256(canonicalize(content.value)) is deterministic across implementations.
Whitespace + key-order mutation of valid-minimal must canonicalize byte-identically (RFC 8785 JCS).
Root keys are deliberately NOT in UTF-16 lexicographic order: proves a conforming verifier must canonicalize before hashing rather than trust source byte order.
alignment.policy.version is a JSON number (3.0) where the schema requires a string; shape validation must reject it.
